Snyk.author: ahartmetz
Snyk.text: Yeah, and "boomslang" means "tree snake". It's not some obscure possibly hip hop related subculture jargon.

Snyk.author: sdoering
Snyk.text: WTF. We have such great medical advancement in cancer treatments, vaccines, reconstructive surgery h just to name a few.Not sure what you are referring to, but the only unjustifiable things in the (so called) medical field are snake oil sales men trying to make a quick buck by instilling a fear of science into people's minds. Like anti-vax idiots. Or homeopathic bullshit.

Snyk.author: benj111
Snyk.text: >Called a ‘snake’ (slang in Dutch)Disappointingly this isn't the origin of the English word slang. It seems to be of Viking origin.

Semgrep.author: khurs
Semgrep.text: The education system is to blame.Kids are conditioned to associate learning with a formal course with a tutor culminating in exams.It's also intentional to segregate skills, if schools taught every child basic plumbing or car mechanics for example instead of spending a month teaching something that won't get used in life, there would be less job in those fields.

CodeQL.author: cadamsdotcom
CodeQL.text: I do not need to waste tokens on skills, I use Claude Code hooks.Have a look at the TDD guard at https://codeleash.dev - the scripts/tdd_log.py arguments are pretty specific but it also has guidance in CLAUDE.md and lots of helpful error messages.

CodeQL.author: pylua
CodeQL.text: Yeah, I agree. It will be like serverless but for code : codeless.It’s a disconcerting future.

CodeQL.author: codeulike
CodeQL.text: Because copper wires could carry enough power to make a landline work when the mains electricity was off due to a power cutEdit: also domestic routers are buggy and unreliable and need to be restarted regularly

CodeQL.author: pjmlp
CodeQL.text: On my hobby coding with C++ I also cross check with CoPilot, alongside the usual VS analysis tools.Which was certainly an improvement, given that Github is in no hurry to add modules support to CodeQL.
